subject: Statistics
title: Voluntary Response Bias
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Voluntary Response Bias.

hook.text: A TV station runs a call-in poll: 'Should taxes be raised?' 95% say no. Is that what the public thinks?

buildup.text: Voluntary response polls attract people with strong opinions. The moderate majority stays silent.

discovery.text: Product reviews work the same way. Thrilled and furious customers write reviews. Satisfied ones don't.

twist.text: Online polls, social media reactions, and comment sections all suffer from this. Loudness isn't prevalence.

climax.text: Self-selected samples measure passion, not population opinion. They're useful — but not representative.

punchline.text: The loudest voices aren't the most common ones.

quiz.question: Why are voluntary response polls unreliable?
options: They attract people with extreme opinions while the moderate majority stays silent
They always have a small sample size
